name: tool_selection
system_prompt: You are a helpful assistant. Choose the best tool for each request.

tools.name: file_write
tools.description: Write or update a local file

tools.name: delegate_agent
tools.description: Delegate to a sub-agent for complex tasks

contrast_types.internal_vs_external: One request needs information from internal/company sources, the other needs external/public web information. Example difference: "our docs" vs "online resources"
contrast_types.local_vs_remote: One request targets local files/resources, the other targets remote/cloud resources. Example difference: "local config" vs "remote server config"
contrast_types.cached_vs_live: One request can be served from cached/stored data, the other requires fresh/live data. Example difference: "last known" vs "current"
contrast_types.read_vs_write: One request is read-only (fetching/viewing), the other requires writing/modification. Example difference: "show me" vs "update the"
contrast_types.create_vs_update: One request creates a new resource, the other modifies an existing one. Example difference: "create a new" vs "modify the existing"
contrast_types.query_vs_mutate: One request queries/reads state, the other mutates/changes state. Example difference: "what is" vs "set the"
contrast_types.single_vs_batch: One request operates on a single item, the other operates on multiple items. Example difference: "this file" vs "all files matching"
contrast_types.shallow_vs_deep: One request needs a surface-level answer, the other needs deep/recursive analysis. Example difference: "quick summary" vs "detailed breakdown"
contrast_types.specific_vs_broad: One request targets a specific item, the other spans a broad scope. Example difference: "this function" vs "the entire module"
contrast_types.authoritative_vs_general: One request needs an authoritative/official source, the other accepts general information. Example difference: "official documentation" vs "any tutorial"
contrast_types.verified_vs_unverified: One request needs verified/trusted data, the other accepts unverified sources. Example difference: "peer-reviewed" vs "blog post"
contrast_types.direct_vs_delegated: One request can be handled directly, the other is complex enough to require delegation/decomposition. Example difference: simple query vs multi-step analysis
contrast_types.single_vs_multi_tool: One request can be solved with a single tool call, the other requires orchestrating multiple tools. Example difference: "look up X" vs "look up X then compare with Y"
